task.goal: Make a structure with the blueprint below
task.initial_team_instruction: Let's share materials and make a structure with the blueprint

replay_context.execution_state_note: cmd_start is reconstructed from each recorded cmd end timestamp minus duration_ms, then revealed to the critic only when the replay clock reaches that start.

message_timeline.local_command_text: !newAction("Complete the blueprint efficiently in survival. Current inventory has gold_block, quartz_block, quartz_pillar, tools; mine nearby stone/granite/andesite/diorite as needed, smelt or craft polished variants and stone_bricks as needed, obtain glowstone if available or craft/use carried if found, then place every listed block exactly at coordinates X -348..-339, Y 64..68, Z 246..255. Prioritize exact blueprint placement, replacing grass/air/wrong blocks as needed, and avoid placing outside the blueprint. When done, run checkBlueprint and fix remaining errors.")

!newAction("Continue building this blueprint now. Use current inventory first: place polished_granite at -348 64 246, polished_andesite wherever required, and polished_diorite wherever required. Then mine/craft stone and stone_bricks locally with the diamond pickaxe, smelting/crafting if needed, and place all stone/stone_bricks requirements. For missing rare blocks gold_block, quartz_block, quartz_pillar, and glowstone, ask nearby bots bob and candy for supplies or collect from any dropped items/chests nearby if available. Place blocks exactly at the listed blueprint coordinates, working level by level Y64 to Y68, avoiding temporary blocks unless needed and removing them afterward. Finish by running a blueprint check and fixing remaining mismatches.")

!newAction("Continue the blueprint build. Count required materials, then gather/craft missing survival blocks where possible: mine stone, granite, andesite, diorite nearby with diamond pickaxe; craft polished_granite, polished_andesite, polished_diorite, and stone_bricks. Place all available stone, stone_bricks, polished variants, and glowstone exactly on blueprint coordinates. For missing gold_block, quartz_block, and quartz_pillar, ask nearby bots and use any supplied blocks; otherwise leave those spots for later. Recheck blueprint when done.")

!newAction("Finish the remaining blueprint fixes. First collect/craft needed materials: mine nearby stone and smelt/craft stone_bricks; mine and craft polished_andesite, polished_diorite, and polished_granite from nearby blocks; use remaining gold_block/quartz_block/quartz_pillar and obtain glowstone if present nearby. Then place all remaining fixes bottom-up: complete every Y64 block first, then Y65, Y66, Y67, Y68, using adjacent support blocks or temporary dirt scaffolding if needed and removing scaffolding afterward. Replace wrong blocks only inside listed blueprint coordinates. Run checkBlueprint at the end and fix any leftovers.")

!newAction("Gather the missing blueprint materials from nearby non-blueprint blocks only, then continue building. Needed types include gold_block, quartz_block, quartz_pillar, glowstone, stone_bricks, stone, polished_andesite, polished_diorite, polished_granite. Do not mine any block inside blueprint bounds X -348..-339, Y 64..68, Z 246..255. Search within 40 blocks for exposed gold_block, quartz_block, quartz_pillar, polished_andesite, polished_diorite, polished_granite, stone, and glowstone; mine them with the diamond pickaxe. Craft stone_bricks from stone if possible and glowstone from glowstone dust if needed. Then place all remaining blueprint blocks exactly at the missing coordinates from the latest check, level by level, and run checkBlueprint afterward.")

observations.claim: All three executors are currently acting and had recent positional activity, so no usable idle capacity is evident at the cutoff.

Inventories show complementary material reductions: Andy lost 23 polished andesite, 14 polished diorite, and 1 polished granite; Bob lost rare quartz/gold materials; Candy lost 26 stone bricks and 15 stone.
Team score rose from 0 to 58.93 and last increased only 1.859 seconds before the cutoff, indicating ongoing team-level progress.

Although the active action scopes overlap broadly, the complementary starting inventories and material reductions are consistent with productive specialization rather than observed wasteful duplication.

agent_assessments.summary: Actively gathering and building after exhausting the polished variants initially held; recent movement and team progress provide no evidence that Andy should be reassigned.
Actively handling remaining fixes with the team's rare gold and quartz stock; those inventories have decreased and Bob was active immediately before cutoff.
The long-running action remains active, with substantial stone and stone-brick reductions and very recent activity; there is no evidence of idleness or a blocked dependency.

limitations: The 911 block changes are unattributed, so individual responsibility for world modifications cannot be established.
All current commands are unfinished at the cutoff; their eventual results and blueprint correctness are unavailable.

Candy's action identifies 14 gold and 9 quartz blocks as missing while Candy has no building blocks; Bob currently holds 14 gold and 11 quartz blocks.
The team score has not increased for 174.698 seconds, despite all three actions remaining active and 1,298 block changes occurring during the window.

The overlapping finish-all scopes and unresolved use of Bob's matching rare-block stock indicate a coordination bottleneck; continued independent execution risks redundant gathering or replacement attempts.

agent_assessments.summary: Actively gathering near the build and now holds 122 cobblestone, but his finish-all scope overlaps both peers rather than defining a distinct stone/crafting role.
Active at the structure and holds the rare-block quantities that match Candy's reported needs, but no explicit handoff or exclusive rare-block assignment is recorded.

manager_advisory.message: At the next safe pause, partition the remaining work: Candy runs one fresh check and publishes missing coordinates; Bob exclusively places gold/quartz; Andy handles stone/stone-brick crafting and placement. Give Candy only a disjoint polished-block set or verification role.

limitations: Active command outcomes are unrevealed at this cutoff, so the score plateau does not prove that each executor made no local progress.

world_changes.attribution_note: Raw replay block changes are not assigned to an executor in this online snapshot.

evidence_boundaries: The snapshot contains no event revealed after window.cutoff_s.
An acting command is execution state, not proof of progress or eventual success.
No active command observed is not sufficient by itself to label an executor idle.
Message recipient and local command executor are separate fields.
Replay block changes remain unattributed unless a separate reliable source identifies the executor.

observations.claim: Andy and Candy are concurrently executing broad, overlapping material-gathering and blueprint-placement actions.
Bob retains 2 gold blocks and 2 quartz blocks, matching the rare-block counts in Andy's latest fix task, after Bob's placement action ended; no new Bob command is active.
Andy holds 225 cobblestone while Candy's active action includes mining cobblestone, obtaining fuel, smelting stone, and crafting stone bricks.

Available inputs are assigned inefficiently: Candy is searching for resources already held by teammates, while all agents pursue substantially the same gather-and-place scope rather than handing off materials or specializing.
The team score has not increased for 142.391 seconds, despite Andy and Candy's ongoing actions and Bob's recently completed action.

agent_assessments.summary: Actively gathering and placing, but his broad scope duplicates Candy's and leaves his large cobblestone stock disconnected from Candy's stone-brick workflow.
His action ended only recently, so idleness is not established; however, he still holds the exact rare-block quantities sought by the active builders.
Candy is actively tasked with a costly broad search and crafting chain while required cobblestone and rare blocks are already held by Andy and Bob.

manager_advisory.message: Stop duplicate searches: have Bob place his 2 gold and 2 quartz at the remaining rare-block coordinates. Have Andy give cobblestone to Candy; Candy should smelt/craft common blocks, while Andy handles polished fixes and runs the next blueprint check.

limitations: The latest revealed blueprint check is from 421.088s and may be stale after subsequent actions.
Andy and Candy are still acting, so their command results and eventual material gains are not yet revealed.
World block changes are unattributed, preventing reliable assignment of construction or destructive actions.
Inventory evidence does not prove that transfer, smelting, or placement is immediately feasible.
